CONTENT.Honours: Paris Saint-GermainLigue 1: 2015–16, 2017–18, 2018–19Trophée des Champions: 2018French GuianaCaribbean Cup third place: 2017
CONTENT.Club career: Rimane trained in the academy programs at Paris Saint-Germain, but signed his first contract, for three years, with Boulogne in the summer of 2011. He played his first professional match for Boulogne on 5 August 2011 against Angers in Ligue 2.Out of contract at Boulogne in 2014, he returned to Paris Saint-Germain B to grow with the reserve and attempt to advance. His faithfulness and his efforts were rewarded with inclusion in the first-team squad for Paris Saint-Germain against Saint-Étienne on 31 January 2016. He started and played the full 90 minutes for Paris Saint-Germain as they defeated Monaco 4–0 in the 2018 Trophée des Champions on 4 August 2018.On 7 February 2019, he signed a loan contract to the end of 2018–19 season with Croatian club NK Istra 1961.Rimane joined Hermannstadt in August 2019.On 2 December 2021, Rimane signed with Championnat National club Bastia-Borgo.On 24 May 2022, Rimane joined Cholet for the 2022–23 season.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 13 May 2022As of match played 6 July 2021As of 9 September 2019. French Guiana score listed first, score column indicates score after each Rimane goal.
CONTENT.International career: Rimane is an international for the French Guiana national team.

SUMMARY: Kévin Ramon Rimane (born 23 February 1991) is a French Guianan professional footballer who plays as a center-back for Championnat National 2 club Borgo and the French Guiana national team.
